_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 10-7 MCA Mutual Aid — Disaster (MDHHS Reviewed 2022-12-27). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Special Operations Protocol Section 10-7: MCA Mutual Aid — Disaster — MDHHS Reviewed 2022
purpose: Establish the protocol authority framework governing EMS providers who respond across MCA (Medical Control Authority) boundaries during declared disasters or mutual aid activations.

sections.title: Scope — When This Protocol Applies
content: This protocol applies when EMS providers and agencies respond OUTSIDE their home MCA's geographic area during:
— State or county declared disaster or emergency
— Formal mutual aid activation (MABAS, EMAC, or local mutual aid agreement)
— Mass casualty incidents requiring cross-MCA resource deployment
— State-directed EMS redeployment under BEPESOC authorization
This protocol does NOT apply to routine transport that crosses MCA lines (e.g., transporting to a hospital in a neighboring MCA)

sections.title: Protocol Authority — Cross-MCA Response
content: When a Michigan EMS provider responds into a neighboring MCA under declared disaster or mutual aid activation:
— The responding EMS agency operates under the PROTOCOLS OF THEIR ORIGINATING (HOME) MCA
— The receiving (host) MCA protocols do NOT supersede the responding agency's home protocols
— Scope of practice is defined by the responding provider's license level and their originating MCA
Example: A Kalamazoo MCA ALS provider responding into Detroit MCA under declared disaster operates under Kalamazoo MCA ALS protocols — not Detroit MCA protocols
Rationale: responders are trained under their own MCA system; imposing an unfamiliar protocol mid-disaster creates patient safety risk

sections.title: Online Medical Control During Cross-MCA Response
content: Online medical control contact: responders should attempt contact with their HOME MCA online medical control first
If home MCA MC is unavailable: contact the HOST MCA online medical control and identify as a mutual aid responder from [home MCA]
Host MCA medical control must be advised that responding agency is operating under originating MCA protocols
For high-risk interventions outside protocol: obtain online MC authorization from home or host MC as available
Document all online MC contacts and authorizations in patient care report

sections.title: Scope of Practice During Cross-MCA Response
content: Provider scope of practice is based on Michigan EMS license level and originating MCA authorizations
MCA Optional interventions: a provider may perform MCA Optional procedures authorized by their HOME MCA even if the HOST MCA has not selected that option
Providers may NOT perform interventions not authorized by their home MCA even if the host MCA has selected them
A higher-level provider cannot be compelled to perform procedures outside their home MCA scope
Providers must carry evidence of their home MCA protocol authorizations if performing MCA Optional procedures during cross-MCA response

sections.title: Documentation Requirements
content: All patient care during cross-MCA disaster response must be documented on standard Michigan PCR
Identify responding agency, home MCA, and incident name on PCR
Submit PCR per normal home agency procedures
Incident Commander maintains agency tracking log for all mutual aid responders on scene
Notify home MCA medical director of cross-MCA activation within 72 hours

sections.title: Liability and Immunity
content: Michigan EMS providers responding under declared disaster or formal mutual aid operate under Michigan EMS Act provisions
Providers acting in good faith under their authorized scope and protocols are afforded standard EMS liability protections
MCL 333.20965 provides additional immunity for good-faith resource coordination decisions during declared emergencies
Document incident declaration number or mutual aid activation reference in PCR when available

deterioration_flags: Unable to reach home MCA online medical control — contact host MCA MC and document; proceed under home MCA standing protocols
Cross-MCA protocol conflict requiring high-risk intervention — err on side of intervention if life-threatening; document and contact MC at first opportunity
scope_notes: During declared disasters and mutual aid activations, responding EMS agencies operate under their originating (home) MCA protocols — NOT the host MCA protocols. This ensures provider familiarity and consistency. Online medical control preference is home MCA; host MCA MC is acceptable alternative when home MC is unreachable.
